title: Partition to K Equal Sum Subsets
coreIdea: Determine if an array can be partitioned into k subsets each with equal sum, by backtracking through subset assignments while using a bitmask to track which elements are already used, pruning branches that can't reach the target sum.
recognitionCues: asks to partition into a fixed number of equal-sum groups
small array size hinted (allows exponential/bitmask approaches)
approach: Compute target sum = total/k (return false if not divisible). Use backtracking with a bitmask of used elements: try adding each unused element to the current subset if it doesn't exceed target; when a subset reaches target exactly, start a new subset (decrement k) and continue. Memoize on bitmask state to avoid recomputation.
timeComplexity: O(k * 2^n) bounded by memoization on bitmask states
